raw_data.details: The “Nishulk Ganavesh Yojana” is a scheme under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free uniforms to all girl students studying in Classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, as well as to all boys belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, and boys from the General category who fall under the Below Poverty Line category.
raw_data.benefits: Under this scheme, two sets of uniforms per student are provided through DBT/SMC to students studying in classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, covering all girls, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe categories, and boys from the General category belonging to BPL families.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. The applicant must be either a girl student, or a boy student belonging to the Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or General Category BPL family. The applicant must be studying in Classes 1 to 8.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineThere is no selection process; all eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 are provided free uniforms or the corresponding financial assistance.
raw_data.documents_required: Documents are required as per the guidelines of the respective school.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityNishulk Ganavesh YojanaUttarakhandNishulk Ganavesh YojanaBPLGirl StudentScheduled CasteScheduled CastesScheduled TribeStudentCheck EligibilityDetailsThe “Nishulk Ganavesh Yojana” is a scheme under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free uniforms to all girl students studying in Classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, as well as to all boys belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, and boys from the General category who fall under the Below Poverty Line category. BenefitsUnder this scheme, two sets of uniforms per student are provided through DBT/SMC to students studying in classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, covering all girls, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe categories, and boys from the General category belonging to BPL families. EligibilityThe applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. The applicant must be either a girl student, or a boy student belonging to the Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or General Category BPL family. The applicant must be studying in Classes 1 to 8. Application ProcessOfflineThere is no selection process; all eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 are provided free uniforms or the corresponding financial assistance. Documents RequiredDocuments are required as per the guidelines of the respective school. Frequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible for the scheme? All girl students of Classes 1 to 8, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories, and boys from General Category BPL families studying in government and government-aided schools are eligible. Which classes are covered under the scheme? The scheme covers students studying in Classes 1 to 8. How many sets of uniforms are provided under this scheme? Each eligible student receives two sets of uniforms every year. Is there any selection process for the scheme? No, there is no selection process. All eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 receive uniforms or related financial assistance. Is the scheme applicable only to government schools? The scheme is applicable to students studying in government and government-aided schools. Are private school students eligible under this scheme? No. Sources And References[Guidelines (Page No. 54)](https://uk.gov.in/department92/library_file/file-30-01-2025-04-25-16.pdf)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: All girl students of Classes 1 to 8, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories, and boys from General Category BPL families studying in government and government-aided schools are eligible.

faqs.question: Which classes are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers students studying in Classes 1 to 8.

faqs.question: How many sets of uniforms are provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Each eligible student receives two sets of uniforms every year.

faqs.question: Is there any selection process for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no selection process. All eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 receive uniforms or related financial assistance.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable only to government schools?
faqs.answer: The scheme is applicable to students studying in government and government-aided schools.

faqs.question: Are private school students eligible under this scheme?
